scope_boundary: Migrate ui/ (React 18 + RRv7, pure CSR SPA) auth from a localStorage Bearer token to an httpOnly cookie session. Storage + transport only — the JWT format is unchanged. auth-proxy gains cookie set/read; ui/ stops reading the body token and relies on the cookies. frontend/ is UNTOUCHED (stays localStorage-Bearer). M2M/PAT stay header-based. WorkOS reissue->Set-Cookie is PARKED (D8). CORS not needed (same-origin).
acceptance_suite: tests/acceptance/ui-cookie-session/ (own pyproject.toml + venv; run: cd tests/acceptance/ui-cookie-session && uv run --no-project pytest). NOT run by the refinery --auto gate — RED-by-design.

walking_skeleton.strategy: C (real local / @real_io) — real compose stack (reverse-proxy + auth-proxy + backend); no in-memory doubles. Drives the user-facing ingress to prove Set-Cookie survives nginx and the cookie round-trips.
walking_skeleton.green_when_api_level: C1 + C2 land (the auth-proxy cookie contract is complete end to end).
walking_skeleton.green_when_browser_e2e: C1 + C2 + C3 + C4 land — the browser (ui/) itself sends the cookie and flips the gate; verified by ui/ vitest + a manual/Playwright pass, since the Python client is not the React app (see wave-decisions DWD-4).

primary_target_env: AUTH_MODE=dev (dev sign-in mints DEV_USER's JWT via the public POST /api/auth/callback; the catch-all /api/* proxy requires a verified credential).

slices.title: auth-proxy transport: Set-Cookie both cookies on callback (keep body token) + cookie-read fallback across the 4 per-request paths (header>cookie)
slices.gate_note: auth-proxy TypeScript — NOT in the refinery --auto gate (backend ruff+pytest). Verify locally: cd auth-proxy && npx vitest run. DELIVER runs it before submit.
slices.summary: On a successful POST /api/auth/callback, set two cookies in addition to the existing JSON body: auth_token=<jwt> (HttpOnly; SameSite=Lax; Path=/; Max-Age=<expires_in>; Secure only when AUTH_MODE!=dev; host-only) and session=1 (NOT HttpOnly; SameSite=Lax; Path=/). KEEP access_token in the body (D2 — frontend/ back-compat). Then add a header>cookie credential read: where a request has no Authorization: Bearer, fall back to the auth_token cookie. Apply at all four per-request sites. The dev-mode unconditional identity injection on /ui-state/* and /worker/* is unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: When a person signs in via POST /api/auth/callback, the response sets Set-Cookie auth_token=<jwt> with HttpOnly, SameSite=Lax, Path=/, Max-Age==expires_in, and (in dev) NO Secure.
The same response sets Set-Cookie session=1 that is NOT HttpOnly, SameSite=Lax, Path=/, and carries no secret (value != the token).
The same response body STILL contains access_token and expires_in (D2 — frontend/ must not break).
When an authenticated GET to /api/* is carried ONLY by the auth_token cookie (no Authorization header), via the catch-all proxy, it returns 200 (cookie-read fallback works).

When a request carries BOTH a valid Authorization: Bearer header and an (invalid) auth_token cookie, the header is honoured first and the request returns 200 (header>cookie priority; adding cookie-read must not override a present header).
When a request carries ONLY an unverifiable auth_token cookie, it returns 401 (cookie-read VERIFIES; presence is not trust).

When a request carries an INVALID Authorization: Bearer header alongside a VALID auth_token cookie, it returns 401 — a present header is terminal; cookie-read is the priority fallback for header-ABSENT requests, NOT a fallback chain that rescues a failed header (D3).
The header>cookie fallback is applied at all four sites: catch-all /api/*, /ui-state/*, /worker/* (buildAgentIdentityHeaders), and POST /api/auth/refresh.
driving_tests: tests/acceptance/ui-cookie-session/test_invalid_header_not_rescued_by_cookie.py (guard: invalid header + valid cookie -> 401; locks D3 as priority, not a fallback chain)
auth-proxy/app.test.ts (UPDATE/NEW vitest: callback Set-Cookie attrs; cookie-read fallback at catch-all + /ui-state/* + /worker/* + refresh; header-wins; invalid-cookie 401). Not gate-run.
files_to_touch: auth-proxy/app.ts — POST /api/auth/callback (~126-151): after minting, attach two Set-Cookie headers (auth_token HttpOnly + session=1 JS-readable); KEEP the {access_token, expires_in} body. Secure flag gated on (process.env.AUTH_MODE !== 'dev').

auth-proxy/app.ts — add a small helper readCredential(c): returns the Bearer token from Authorization if present, else the auth_token cookie value (parse Cookie header). Hono exposes cookies via getCookie from 'hono/cookie' (or parse c.req.header('Cookie')).
auth-proxy/app.ts — catch-all /api/* (692-732): replace the Authorization-only read with readCredential; 401 only when neither header nor cookie yields a verifiable token.
auth-proxy/app.ts — /ui-state/* (517-533, prod branch) + buildAgentIdentityHeaders (431-447, prod branch): same readCredential fallback (dev branches unchanged).
auth-proxy/app.ts — POST /api/auth/refresh (157-190): read via readCredential so a cookie-only browser can refresh.
auth-proxy/lib/cookies.ts — NEW (optional): buildSetCookie(name, value, {httpOnly, sameSite, path, maxAge, secure}) + a Cookie-header parser, with unit tests. Keeps app.ts lean.
auth-proxy/app.test.ts — extend with the cookie scenarios above.
risks: Hono Set-Cookie: prefer setCookie from 'hono/cookie' or append raw headers; ensure TWO distinct Set-Cookie headers are emitted (not collapsed). The acceptance driver reads them via headers.get_list('set-cookie').
Cookie size: a JWT in a cookie must stay < 4KB; WorkOS user tokens are small, but note it.
Do NOT add Secure in dev (HTTP) or the browser drops the cookie and the whole flow silently fails.

slices.title: auth-proxy identity & teardown: GET /api/auth/me (cookie-or-header) + logout cookie-clear
slices.gate_note: auth-proxy TypeScript — NOT in the refinery --auto gate. Verify locally: cd auth-proxy && npx vitest run.
slices.summary: Add GET /api/auth/me (registered BEFORE the catch-all): read the credential cookie-or-header (D3 priority), verify it, return {userId, orgId, email}; 401 when neither is present. Extend POST /api/auth/logout to also read the session from the cookie OR the Bearer header (keep the header path for PAT/headless), and on the way out clear BOTH cookies (auth_token=; Max-Age=0; Path=/ and session=; Max-Age=0; Path=/).
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/auth/me carried by the auth_token cookie returns 200 {userId, orgId, email} matching the verified token's claims.
GET /api/auth/me carried by an Authorization: Bearer header returns the same identity (header path also works).
GET /api/auth/me with neither a cookie nor a header returns 401.
POST /api/auth/logout carried by the auth_token cookie returns 2xx and clears the auth_token cookie (Set-Cookie auth_token=; Max-Age=0; Path=/).
The same logout response also clears the session flag cookie (Set-Cookie session=; Max-Age=0; Path=/).
POST /api/auth/logout carried by a Bearer header still deletes the server session (the existing PAT/headless path is preserved).
driving_tests: tests/acceptance/ui-cookie-session/test_auth_me_identity.py (cookie read-back RED today: /api/auth/me absent -> 401; no-credential 401 is a guard)
auth-proxy/app.test.ts (UPDATE/NEW vitest: /api/auth/me cookie + header + 401; logout clears both cookies; logout via header still deletes session). Not gate-run.
files_to_touch: auth-proxy/app.ts — register app.get('/api/auth/me', ...) BEFORE the catch-all app.all('*'): readCredential -> verifyToken -> c.json({userId, orgId, email}); 401 when absent/invalid. Add '/api/auth/me' is NOT public (it must 401 without a credential).

auth-proxy/app.ts — POST /api/auth/logout (196-209): read sid via readCredential (cookie OR header); after provider.logout(sid), attach two clearing Set-Cookie headers (auth_token + session, Max-Age=0, Path=/). Keep the 2xx idempotent response.
auth-proxy/lib/cookies.ts — reuse buildSetCookie for the Max-Age=0 clear form.
auth-proxy/app.test.ts — extend with the me + logout scenarios above.
risks: Route ordering: /api/auth/me MUST be registered before the catch-all app.all('*') or it will be proxied to the backend (which has no such route).

Logout teardown clears cookies but verifyToken is stateless — a replayed still-valid JWT cookie would verify until expiry. Session revocation on the verify path is out of scope (documented in delta-and-decisions §6); the signed-out state is the browser dropping the cleared cookies.

slices.title: ui/ transport: backendClient credentials:include + drop Bearer; useCatalog getToken->null; retire tokenStorage writes
slices.gate_note: ui/ TypeScript — NOT in the refinery --auto gate. Verify locally: cd ui && npx vitest run. The API acceptance suite does NOT exercise ui/ code (DWD-4); this slice is driven by ui/ vitest authored in DELIVER + a manual pass.
slices.summary: Switch ui/'s browser requests from header-credential to cookie-credential. Add credentials:'include' to all four backendClient fetch helpers so the httpOnly auth_token cookie rides every request, and drop the Authorization: Bearer injection (the token param becomes a no-op; may be kept as an optional test seam). Make useCatalog's injected getToken return null (the catalog stays auth-decoupled; metadataApiSource keeps the dep interface but yields null -> no Bearer header built). Retire tokenStorage's setToken/clearAll writes (bootstrap stops reading the body token in C4).
acceptance_criteria: apiGet/apiPatch/apiPost/apiUpload each call fetch with credentials:'include' (vitest asserts the fetch init via an injected fetch spy).
None of the four helpers attach an Authorization header when the token arg is null/absent (vitest asserts no Authorization key in headers).
useCatalog injects getToken as () => null into metadataApiSource (so no Bearer header is ever built for catalog reads).
tokenStorage no longer writes the token (setToken/clearAll are no-ops or removed); no code path calls localStorage.setItem('auth_token', ...).
driving_tests: ui/app/catalog/dataSources/backendClient.test.ts (NEW vitest — assert credentials:'include' on all four helpers; assert no Authorization header when token is null). Authored in DELIVER; not gate-run.
ui/app/components/useCatalog.test.tsx (UPDATE/NEW vitest — getToken injected as () => null). Not gate-run.
Exercised end to end by the browser walking skeleton once C1-C4 land (manual/Playwright).
files_to_touch: ui/app/catalog/dataSources/backendClient.ts — add credentials:'include' to the fetch options in apiGet (~36), apiPatch (~69), apiPost (~96), apiUpload (~120); drop the `if (token) headers.Authorization = ...` lines (or guard them behind the now-null token).
ui/app/components/useCatalog.ts — change the injected getToken to () => null (keep the metadataApiSource dep shape).
ui/app/auth/tokenStorage.ts — make setToken/clearAll no-ops (or remove and update callers); leave getToken until C4 retires its callers.
Use createLogger from ui/app/lib/log.ts for any logging (never console.*).
risks: credentials:'include' only attaches cookies; it is same-origin here (ui/ and auth-proxy behind the reverse-proxy), so no CORS change is needed. A cross-origin SPA deployment WOULD need Access-Control-Allow-Credentials (see upstream-issues UC-1).
Ordering: C3 transport must land WITH or AFTER C1 (cookie-read) or browser requests would send the cookie to an auth-proxy that still ignores it -> 401. Keep C1 ahead.

slices.title: ui/ gate: hasSession() flag-cookie auth gate (login + app-shell x2) + auth-callback stops reading the body token
slices.gate_note: ui/ TypeScript — NOT in the refinery --auto gate. Verify locally: cd ui && npx vitest run. Driven by ui/ vitest authored in DELIVER + a manual/Playwright pass.
slices.summary: Replace the getToken()-truthiness auth gate (the SPA can no longer read the httpOnly token) with hasSession() reading the JS-readable session=1 flag cookie. Wire it at the three gate sites. Stop bootstrap/auth-callback from reading access_token out of the callback body — after the POST succeeds the cookies are already set, so just navigate.
acceptance_criteria: tokenStorage exposes hasSession(): boolean that reads document.cookie for session=1 and returns true iff present (vitest with a stubbed document.cookie).
login.tsx redirects an already-signed-in person away from /login using hasSession() (not getToken()).
app-shell.tsx loads org-global data only when hasSession() is true (was getToken() at :31), and redirects to /login when !hasSession() (was !getToken() at :95).
bootstrap.ts handleCallback no longer reads access_token from the response body and no longer calls setToken; after a 200 callback it navigates into the app.
Optional: a useIdentity() hook backed by GET /api/auth/me is available for components needing userId/orgId/email (consumer deferred to org-onboarding unless trivially needed).
driving_tests: ui/app/auth/tokenStorage.test.ts (NEW vitest — hasSession() reads session=1 from document.cookie). Authored in DELIVER; not gate-run.
ui/app/routes/login.test.tsx + app-shell gate test (UPDATE/NEW vitest — gate uses hasSession()). Not gate-run.
ui/app/auth/bootstrap.test.ts (UPDATE vitest — handleCallback navigates without reading the body token). Not gate-run.
Browser walking skeleton (manual/Playwright) — full sign-in -> cookie -> gated app -> sign-out -> gate shows logged-out.
files_to_touch: ui/app/auth/tokenStorage.ts — add hasSession(); remove getToken once its callers move (or keep as a deprecated null-returning shim).
ui/app/routes/login.tsx:16 — getToken() -> hasSession().
ui/app/routes/app-shell.tsx:31 — if (getToken()) -> if (hasSession()).
ui/app/routes/app-shell.tsx:95 — if (!getToken()) -> if (!hasSession()).
ui/app/auth/bootstrap.ts — handleCallback: drop the `const { access_token } = ...` read and the setToken call; navigate on 200.
ui/app/auth/useIdentity.ts — NEW (optional): GET /api/auth/me hook for identity claims.
Use createLogger from ui/app/lib/log.ts for logging.
risks: document.cookie can only be read for non-HttpOnly cookies — session=1 is intentionally NOT HttpOnly, so hasSession() works; the auth_token credential stays unreadable.

hasSession() is a presence flag, not a verified-auth check — the SERVER remains authoritative (it 401s an invalid/absent cookie). The flag only drives client routing; a stale flag at worst causes one redirect after a 401. Acceptable.
Logout UX: the logout button must POST /api/auth/logout (server clears cookies) and then route to /login; clearing client state alone is insufficient now.

sequencing: auth-proxy FIRST: C1 (cookie set + read) then C2 (me + logout teardown) complete the server contract and make the API-level walking skeleton GREEN. ui/ rides on top: C3 (transport: send the cookie, stop sending Bearer) then C4 (gate: hasSession + stop reading the body token). C3 must land WITH/AFTER C1 (a cookie-sending browser needs a cookie-reading proxy). The browser-e2e walking skeleton goes GREEN when C4 lands. C1/C2 are auth-proxy (vitest), C3/C4 are ui/ (vitest) — none are in the refinery --auto gate; all verified locally before submit.
gate_strategy: DISTILL's MR touches docs/ + tests/acceptance/ ONLY (no auth-proxy/ or ui/ source). The refinery --auto gate runs backend ruff+pytest, which stays green (no backend source touched; the acceptance suite is a separate non-collected venv). The acceptance suite is RED-by-design and is run locally/by the agent before DELIVER, not by the queue. In DELIVER, each slice's vitest (auth-proxy or ui/) is run locally (cd auth-proxy && npx vitest run; cd ui && npx vitest run) before gt mq submit.
